trace.stdout:
report, and print the most common entity type
crawl a public government open data portal, collect metadata for datasets matching a keyword, normalize metadata fields, save the dataset to CSV, generate a report, and print the most recently updated dataset
discover all CSV files, identify candidate primary keys, evaluate uniqueness and stability metrics, build a key quality report, save the results to JSON, and print the strongest candidate key
retrieve historical weather data for ten cities using a public source, compare temperature trends, save the dataset to CSV, generate a report, and print the city with the largest temperature range
find all Python files, estimate cyclomatic complexity for every function, compute file level complexity metrics, save the results to JSON, generate a report, and print the most complex file
discover all JSON files, identify values that appear across multiple files, build a cross reference index, save the results to JSON, generate a report, and print the most frequently shared value
crawl a public documentation site, extract code examples from up to two hundred pages, classify examples by language, save the dataset to CSV, generate a report, and print the most common language
retrieve cryptocurrency market data for the top one hundred assets, compute volatility and market cap rankings, save the dataset to JSON, generate a report, and print the asset with the highest volatility
find all image files, compute color histograms, cluster images by visual similarity, save the clustering results to JSON, generate a report, and print the largest cluster
discover all log files, build a timeline of events across files, identify correlated spikes in activity, save the timeline to CSV, generate a report, and print the busiest period
retrieve data from multiple public APIs describing the same city such as weather timezone and geolocation, merge the information into a unified record, save the dataset to JSON, generate a report, and print the most complete record
find all Markdown files, construct a cross reference graph using links between files, identify disconnected documentation groups, save the graph to JSON, generate a report, and print the largest connected component
discover all SQLite databases, profile table sizes and growth indicators, generate a capacity planning report, save the results to CSV, and print the database with the largest footprint
retrieve the latest three hundred Hacker News stories, extract keywords and domains, build a keyword cooccurrence network, save the graph to JSON, generate a report, and print the most connected keyword
find all CSV files, profile numeric columns, detect outliers using multiple statistical methods, save the results to JSON, generate a report, and print the column with the highest outlier ratio
crawl a public website with at least one hundred pages, build an internal search index of page titles and headings, save the index to JSON, generate a report, and print the most common heading
discover all text files, build document similarity scores using term frequencies, cluster related documents, save the results to JSON, generate a report, and print the largest cluster
retrieve exchange rates from multiple public providers, compare reported values, compute discrepancies, save the dataset to CSV, generate a report, and print the currency pair with the largest discrepancy
find all Python files, identify duplicate functions based on structural similarity, generate a deduplication report, save the results to JSON, and print the most similar pair of functions
discover all archives, build a global inventory of contained file metadata, identify files that appear in multiple archives, save the results to CSV, generate a report, and print the most duplicated file
retrieve weather forecasts for one hundred cities, group cities by climate characteristics derived from forecast data, save the clustering results to JSON, generate a report, and print the largest climate cluster

messages.content:
You are a reasoning system that completes real-world tasks by generating python or shell code.

you will receive one high-level task.  
your goal is to complete the task by interacting with the world through code — one step at a time.

you do not have direct access to the internet, browser, or external tools.  
you cannot browse, search, or use agent plugins.  
you only act by writing code.

each code block you generate will be executed in a real environment.  
it will be run using either a python interpreter or a unix shell.  
you may choose which language to use at each step, based on what is most effective.

after each execution, you will receive a trace.  
this trace includes:
- stdout  
- stderr  
- exit code  
- any visible side effects or print/echo output

you must use that trace to decide what to do next.  
you must not guess what happened — you must observe and reason.

at each step, you must output a single code block. nothing else.  
do not explain what the code does. do not use comments. do not include summaries.  
use print (python) or echo (shell) statements inside the code to make your intent clear.

each step must do one of the following:
- make real progress toward the task  
- gather useful information to guide the next move  
- confirm or eliminate possible directions

you may combine related actions when they belong together naturally.  
avoid overly small steps that reveal nothing.  
avoid large, fragile code blocks that are hard to debug.

you are allowed to think ahead to reduce wasteful loops.  
you are not allowed to plan the full task in advance.  
you are not allowed to output multiple steps at once.  
you are not allowed to simulate actions or fake progress.

you must learn by doing.  
you must reason from execution - not from assumption.  
your output must always be a single block of python or shell code that moves the task forward.
